expected_trades_per_day_basis: pricechange channel emits ~120 events/day; basis_rate>=0.001 AND premium_bps>=20 AND move_pct>=3 is a triple-conjunction gate — empirically ~2-3% of pricechange pumps satisfy all three simultaneously (basis dislocation requires funding-cycle stress + active perp premium + meaningful move), yielding ~2.8 triggers/day before symbol/cooldown dedup.

best_in: funding-rate divergence regimes (basis_rate above session 90th percentile)
retail-driven altcoin pump-and-fade days where perp leads spot by >20bps
high-OI symbols where premium decay is mechanically forced by funding settlement
worst_in: BTC-led structural rallies where basis stays stretched for hours (no mean-reversion)
low-volume periods where premium_bps>=20 reflects thin book rather than real dislocation
pre-funding-flip windows where basis is about to invert and short pays funding
regime_sensitivity.explanation: The thesis is perp-spot dislocation fade: when basis AND premium are simultaneously extreme on a pump, the perp is mechanically overpriced relative to spot and funding pressure should compress the gap. This works in mean-reverting altcoin pumps but fails when macro flow keeps basis stretched (BTC bull legs) or when the basis signal is noise from illiquidity rather than real positioning.

live_readiness_caveats: Basis_rate and premium_bps must be computed from the SAME 1-minute bar as the trigger — staleness >30s will cause entries after the dislocation has already collapsed (the edge decays in seconds, not minutes).

Symbol liquidity filter is mandatory: premium_bps>=20 on micro-caps (top-200 by volume) reflects book thinness, not real dislocation — restrict to top-50 perp pairs by 24h volume to avoid false positives.
Funding settlement windows (00:00/08:00/16:00 UTC ±5min) should be EXCLUDED — basis collapses mechanically at those times regardless of strategy thesis, contaminating attribution.

monitoring_metrics.metric: Spearman(basis_rate, premium_bps) on triggered events, rolling 7-day
monitoring_metrics.rationale: Synthesizer flagged this — if the two filter dimensions become collinear, the 'joint signal' collapses to a single-factor trigger and the archetype is a duplicate of premium-fade shorts (E-class baseline). Below 0.85 confirms genuine joint novelty.

monitoring_metrics.metric: Median time-to-mean-reversion (seconds from entry until basis_rate returns to <0.0005)
monitoring_metrics.rationale: The thesis depends on mechanical funding-driven compression. If the half-life stretches beyond 5 minutes, the perp dislocation is being absorbed by sustained directional flow, not mean-reversion — edge has decayed.

Triple-conjunction filters are notoriously brittle to data-distribution drift. Frequency drift in either direction signals filter calibration is off (drift up = thresholds too loose post-regime-shift; drift down = market structure changed and basis dislocations are no longer triggering).

monitoring_metrics.metric: Realized fill slippage (bps) vs backtest assumption
monitoring_metrics.rationale: Shorts on pumps face borrow availability + adverse-selection in maker book. If slippage exceeds backtest by >15bps, the bounded mean-reversion edge (likely 30-50bps gross) is half-erased before exit kernel fires.

monitoring_metrics.metric: Concurrent open positions (same archetype, different symbols)
monitoring_metrics.rationale: Basis dislocations cluster across correlated alts during sector pumps — a 'safe' diversified short can become a single concentrated bet on funding-rate compression failing. Cap exposure to avoid tail correlation.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: 7-day rolling mean trade return flips negative AND basis_rate-to-mean-reversion median half-life >300s for 5 consecutive days
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Joint condition isolates regime change (mean-reversion thesis broken) from noise. Either alone is recoverable; both together means perp-spot mechanics have shifted (likely sustained directional bid) and the archetype's core thesis is invalidated.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: Maximum drawdown on paper account exceeds 8% within first 14 days
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: R2's E126 lesson: asymmetric short trades can compound surprisingly fast even with positive backtest score. 8% on $1000 ($80) is 1.6× the 5% per-trade sizing cap, indicating multiple back-to-back losers — symmetric geometry claim from synthesizer is failing in live.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: Realized win-rate drops below 55% over 30 trades AND median winner < median loser in absolute terms
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Mean-reversion shorts must win frequently with bounded gains; if win-rate degrades while loss magnitude grows, the geometry has flipped from the synthesizer's 'naturally symmetric' assumption to the E126-class trap (rare but catastrophic SL hits).

expected_max_dd_basis: At 2.8 trades/day × 30 days = ~84 trades, with synthesizer-claimed symmetric geometry and 5% sizing, worst-case 4-5 consecutive losers at ~1.5% each compounds to ~6-7% — adjusted upward from a naive 5% to account for short-side adverse-selection and basis-thesis regime risk highlighted in worst_in regimes.
compare_to_existing: E126 pc_pump_taker_buy_extreme_long (inverse side, same channel, same pump-context) — triangulate by checking whether E213's short triggers cluster on the SAME events where E126 longs would have fired; if overlap >40%, this is a contrarian fade of E126's signal and should be paper-traded as a hedged pair to expose true edge separation rather than a standalone short.
